prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and vascular risk factors

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to disability and caregiver burden
burden.mortalityRate: Moderate (driven by comorbidity and stroke risk)

vulnerablePopulations: low-income populations with poor vascular risk control
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Cognitive decline with vascular events or imaging evidence; neuroimaging supports diagnosis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited cognitive screening in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; depends on vascular risk management

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical assessment with imaging in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Vascular risk factor control, antiplatelet therapy when indicated, cognitive support and caregiver education
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to neuroimaging and dementia support services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing chronic disease programs for hypertension/diabetes
